ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for multiple Heirs. Alma Beck for multiple Heirs. Steven Weinkauf for Petitioner. Veronica Chico in Propria Persona. John Mangini also present.

Mr. Weinkauf stated that Notice of Proposed Action regarding the bid on the jewelery as well as another Notice of Proposed Action accepting the bid was provided with no response. Ms. Chico expressed her desire to purchase the jewelry and stated that she placed a higher bid. The Court ordered that the jewelry be sold to Ms. Kinlaw and suggested that Ms. Chico contact Ms. Kinlaw to see if they could work something out. Mr. Weinkauf addressed the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes. Counsel agree on Mr. Zamora's interest being divided into thirteen equal share to his Spouse and 12 Children. Discussion was held with regard to the October 2012 order needing to be corrected. Mr. Weinkauf stated that Marie Bender Guel passed away in El Dorado. The Court informed Mr. Wienkauf that original 13100 Declarations signed by the Trustee need to be filed as well as Rafael Estrella's 13100 Declaration. The Court stated that Helene's share is to be set aside and that the birth certificate needs to be filed. Mr. Weinkauf is to deliver all eleven boxes to Attorney Mark Bridges. Counsel will allow the Heirs to determine the method of receiving the funds (Western Union or wire transfer). The Court will authorize payment to the Heirs and the Heirs could authorize payment to their Power of Attorney (the Court indicated that something in writing would be helpful). The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to receipt and review of a proposed order). Mr. Weinkauf is to prepare the order which should indicate the Heir, the fractional interest, the amount to be paid to any assignee or the Alameda Department of Child Support, and show a total.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Helen Yune-Trowbridge. Bailiff: Deputy M. Loos. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for Petitioner. Alma Beck for 40 Paternal Heirs. David Owens for Pamela Garcia. Sean Kenney for American German Heir Search. Janet Boessenecker for 90 Heirs. John Mangini for Robert Serrano and Mary Riewerts.

The Court approved the Petition to Determine Persons Entitled to Distribution of Estate and Petition for Instructions (subject to receipt and review of a revised order). The order is to state and identify the 17 Individuals with no proof of heirship and the 3 Individuals with incomplete proof of heirship. Their shares are to be set aside and if no proof of Heirship has been provided at time of final distribution, the shares are to be deposited with the County Treasurer. Mr. Weinkauf is to prepare a revised order reflecting the above.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Marie Bender Guel, Veronica Chico and Gary Canonica (Interested Parties) also appeared in Propria Persona.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the disposition of the household property, missing items, fees, accessibilty to the abandoned boxes, and the manner in which the estate was being administered. The Interested Parties will meet with Mr. Weinkauff/Public Administator on January 14, 2010 between 1:00 p.m. and 4:00 p.m. to look at the boxes. The boxes are to be made available. The matter was taken under submission. Letters from some of the other Interested Parties were submitted to the Court.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

The Court stated that it was not in a position to do anything until all of the Heirs have been determined and that someone needed to file a petition pursuant to Probate Code 11700. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the personal property, the storage fees and the Heirs. The Court directed Mr. Weinkauff to contact the auction house directing them to allow Marie Bender Guel to look at the home furnishings but not be allowed to take anything. The Court ordered the following: 1)a public auction of the home furnishings 2)that notice be given to all of the known Heirs 3)the pictures, the jewelery and the boxes of tax returns are to be stored by the Administrator and 4)the 11 abondoned boxes are to be given to Marie Bender Guel. Mr. Weinkauff will prepare an order. The matter was continued to December 8, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status.

Petition Of Marie T. Bender Guel In Opposition To The Petition For Letters Of Administration & Authority To Admin Estate
Off Calendar Jan-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Tim Beresky for Jeannie Ziegele (Petitioner). Marie Bender Guel in propria persona. Peter Myers for Multiple Heirs. David Owens for Multiple Heirs. Scott Jordan for Multiple Heirs. Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch.

Mr. Beresky stated that he filed the petition to move the matter along, that Marie Bender Guel filed a petition but was denied appointment as the Conservator, that he contacted Ms. Dolch, that Ms. Dolch has now petitioned the Court to serve, that the Public Administrator then jumped in, and that his clients should serve or in the alternative, Ms. Dolch. Mr. Owens and Ms. Boessenecker stated that they were in support of Ms. Dolch being appointed. Marie Bender Guel presented a brief history of her care/concern for Mr. Panol, stated that she had attempted to submit a packet of information to the Court, and noted that Mr. Beretsky didn't respond to inquires made by her or other heirs. The Court stated it would be best in this instance to appoint a neutral, either Debra Dolch or the Public Administrator. Ms. Bender Guel stated she wished to nominate Gary Konica. The Court stated that nomination would not achieve his goal of appointing an uninterested party. Gloria Griego, Anita Rosas, Ms. Riewerts, Genevieve Willingham, and Manuel Herrera (various family memmbers/interested parties) all stated their individual concerns for the record. Ms. Guel stated that if she wasn't appointed she was in support of Mr. Weinkauf and that she would be willing to work closely with him. Mr. Jordan stated he was opposed to Mr. Beresky's client and that he was in support of Ms. Dolch's appointment. Mr. Beresky responsed to comments on the record. Ms. Rasch stated that they were asked to file, and that her client was prepared to serve. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
